TextENGNew: "Excuse me."
"You called for me?"
"Yeah.@k@vS11/30/420200199. Here, have a seat."
"Okay..."
At his prompting, Tomoe lowered herself onto the reception sofa.
changeReason: Adds 'reception sofa' (応接ソファ from JPN) establishing the formal office setting, whereas old just says 'the sofa.'

TextENGNew: The distinctive scent of leather and the soft, supple texture settled around her — it was surprisingly comfortable.
(Come to think of it... I've never actually sat in this sofa before.)
That was probably because she never wanted to linger in the chief's office. She had always received Yamaoki's orders and communications while standing.
Yamaoki stood with his back to her, gazing out the window, a cigarette trailing smoke between his fingers.
...Something felt slightly off. Tomoe had never known him to smoke.
"Sorry to keep you waiting.@k@vS11/30/420200201. ...Now, on to the main subject."
With that preamble, Yamaoki picked up a sheet of paper from his desk and placed it on the table before Tomoe.
It was a single-page letter.
The name written there was... 'Tomoe Minai.' And—
"Wh... what is this?!"
Tomoe shot to her feet, the letter still clutched in her hand, eyes wide with shock as she read and reread its contents.
The cold, impersonal characters refused to resolve into meaning. ...No — she simply didn't want them to.
Fury and confusion blazed through her mind like fire, and a sensation like vertigo blurred her thoughts and her vision.
If only she were permitted, she would tear this letter to shreds right here and now... It took an enormous effort to suppress that urge.

"According to the contents,@k@vS11/30/420200203. the National Police Agency's public relations office has requested that you be assigned to serve as the specially appointed chief of media correspondence.@k@vS11/30/420200204. ...In brief, that's what it says."
With the recent expansion of mass media, information about criminal cases had become instantly accessible to ordinary citizens.
Depending on how events unfolded, this posed the risk of spreading reputational damage to victims, perpetrators, and their associates — and could psychologically pressure suspects into rash action.
changeReason: Old translation omits the second half of the JPN text about psychologically pressuring suspects into rash action (犯人を心理的に圧迫して性急を促す); new translation includes this information.

TextENGNew: However, if the police provided no information at all, each media outlet would simply fabricate articles based on speculation and conjecture, flooding the public sphere with misinformation and misunderstanding.

For this reason, it had become urgent for the National Police Agency to establish a specialized body that could both regulate information released to the press and provide curated, controlled disclosures.
However...
"H-hold on!@k@vS11/28/420101251. It's only been two months since I arrived at Kakiuchi Station!@k@vS11/28/420101252. Isn't it unreasonable for personnel changes to happen so rapidly?!"

"Although there is a maximum duration for the training period, there is no designated minimum.@k@vS11/30/420200206. The training period can end as soon as the acting head at their temporary location determines that the trainee has accomplished their training goals."
In fact, even though she was only there for two months, Tomoe had achieved far more than Yamaoki and the others expected.
...In conventional investigation, the detective's primary activity is utilizing their intuition and experience.

As a result, differences in investigators' abilities can lead to multiple oversights and duplication of information. Upper management and on-site investigators were interested in improving that inefficiency.

Under those circumstances, it was no exaggeration at all to say that the profiling analysis techniques that Tomoe pioneered gave investigators relevant guidelines and valuable clues to solve those problems.
That development was highly praised within the National Police Agency, and further research and testing will be conducted from now on, leading to potential full-scale adoption down the road...
"I never expected you to achieve so much in such a short period of time.@k@vS11/30/420200208. You should be proud of your accomplishments."

"Enough with the flattery!@k@vS11/28/420101254. Besides, shouldn't police bureaucrats handle correspondence with the media rather than the detectives themselves?@k@vS11/28/420101255. That's outside my line of work!"
"It may be impossible for desk workers to properly determine the subset of investigation information fit for disclosure."

"Not to mention, considering you've experienced this from the victim's point of view, you of all people should be able to understand their perspectives and know what's needed to prevent rumors from causing any secondary damage to their reputations."
"Upper management has decided that you're the best possible candidate to meet both of those qualifications."
"W-well...!@k@vS11/28/420101257. That's true, but I...!"

"Your opinion is not part of the debate here.@k@vS11/30/420200213. ...I'll say it again, this is an order.@k@vS11/30/420200214. If you're a public servant, it's your responsibility to follow orders from above."

Tomoe was still upset that she didn't have a chance to argue her side of the story... but she understood that fact and kept quiet. All civil servants, regardless of organization, had no right to defy a direct order for personal reasons.
(But still...!)
There were still a large number of unresolved cases under investigation. Passing that on to the other detectives at the station would cause unnecessary hardship for them.
Not to mention... she still hadn't grasped the truth behind the Nagisa Ozaki case.
Even if she wholeheartedly wanted to pursue the culprit, she'd never be able to solve anything if she wasn't working as a detective actively investigating the case...
The girl who said Tomoe was "kind-hearted" and thanked her for all her help.
When they last met, the girl was trying her hardest to believe she could reconcile with her old friend.
But it was impossible for her to set things right, since her untimely death took her before she could accomplish anything.
Plus, Tomoe had already identified a clearly fishy suspect that she was hoping to investigate.
And yet...!
"Please... Please, Chief!"
So.
Even though it was nearly impossible to overturn a decision like this, if there was any hope of doing so Tomoe was willing to bet on it.
"Give me just a little longer... Three months, even one month would be enough!@k@vS11/28/420101263. Can you give me more time?"
"It can't end this way. I don't want it to end like this!@k@vS11/28/420101265. There's still something I want to do... something I have to do!"
"Fujita-kun told me about the incident in Oumaki.@k@vS11/30/420200216. ...So, I understand how you feel."
"Then, at least...!"
"...Do you think you can accomplish something in a month?"
"What is your basis for that?@k@vS11/30/420200219. How are you so confident?@k@vS11/30/420200220. ...Don't say something so presumptuous, Minai-kun."
"Even if you struggle as hard as you can on your own, you may not accomplish anything. And you might even break yourself in the process."
"...Maybe you'll be able to give yourself a pat on the back for giving it your all, but in the end it's the same as not getting results.@k@vS11/30/420200223. You're just making excuses for yourself."
"You can't know that for sure unless we give it a try!!"
Tomoe slammed the table in front of her while leaning closer to Yamaoki.
Why...?
Why is he exerting so much pressure to defy me this time?!
What does he know about me?!
"I won't let someone like you who hasn't even tried tell me that!@k@vS11/28/420101269. I don't want you to tell it to me!!@k@vS11/28/420101270. What do you know? What?!"
"A girl with a hopeful future, who finally found the means to be happy, died without her thoughts ever coming to fruition!@k@vS11/28/420101272. She was murdered!"
"Even as a detective, I couldn't do anything for her!@k@vS11/28/420101274. And it's impossible for me to do anything in the future!!@k@vS11/28/420101275. That's why I... I...!!"
"...That's the definition of self-satisfaction, Tomoe-kun."
Yamaoki quietly stared at the rage in Tomoe's eyes.
Her tremendous sorrow and frustration were insufficient to sway his heart.

"Even if you catch the culprit, what has been lost will never return.@k@vS11/30/420200226. And it won't fill the sense of loss in the victims and their families unless you repay what they've lost.@k@vS11/30/420200227. In other words... the victim's life."
And needless to say... the souls of the dead can never be brought back.
So, unless you're a god... it's impossible to fully heal the wounds of the people left behind.
"...So, what you're trying to do right now is ultimately meaningless. It won't create any value for anyone.@k@vS11/30/420200229. Or am I wrong?"

"...But what about justice?!@k@vS11/28/420101278. Isn't that enough reason for the police to keep going and capture the culprit?!@k@vS11/28/420101279. Weren't you the one who taught me that, Yamaoki-san?!"

"Indeed I did say that.@k@vS11/30/420200231. But what you're describing is your own personal justice, not the justice of the entire police force.@k@vS11/30/420200232. And the justice of the police could very well contradict your own judgment.@k@vS11/30/420200233. ...So, you need to accept this."
Then... what?
Then, the justice I believe in... is different from that of other people? ...Is that what you want to say?!
The blood rushed to Tomoe's head. She suddenly leaned toward Yamaoki, with every last bit of her strength focused on clenching her fists.
...However, Yamaoki quietly gazed at her while calmly enduring her violent outburst.
"Calm down, Tomoe-kun.@k@vS11/30/420200235. Your thoughts are getting hasty and overly emotional."

"It's fine to have heroic aspirations, but you're saying your own beliefs are justice, when there are as many definitions of justice as there are people.@k@vS11/30/420200237. ...You need to think more about what we, the entire police force, should do."
She... couldn't say anything in protest.
There was no meaning to revenge. ...Yamaoki had remonstrated her as much the other day.
And media correspondence at the public relations office was more important than her involvement on this one particular case... That must've been what they determined.
But then, how would she fill the empty void in her heart?
How could she suppress the urge to be swallowed by the darkness of hatred and despair?
And on top of that...!
There was... one more thing that Tomoe still couldn't understand.

"Why didn't you refuse this request?@k@vS11/28/420101284. You were the one who nominated me for the recommended group, so you could've refused even after meeting with the public relations office, couldn't you?!@k@vS11/28/420101285. And yet, why?!"
Yamaoki kept silent and didn't change his expression in response to Tomoe's fierce question. No matter how frustrated she was with him, she still had to ask.
"...I heard you badgered the director of public security last year to get me transferred to Kakiuchi!"
"And I came to the place my father mentioned in his final note over a decade ago, expecting to finally grasp something...@k@vS11/28/420101288. I've been thinking about that this whole time!"
"Tomoe-kun..."
"So, I was thrilled that you called me to Kakiuchi!@k@vS11/28/420101290. I was certain you had given me the chance to solve my father's final mystery!"
"I was happy to be near Madoka and part of the recommended group, but more than that I was truly grateful for the opportunity you gave me!"
"...And yet, why are you trying to send me away from Kakiuchi?!@k@vS11/28/420101293. What the hell did you bring me here for?!"
That's right, this was Kakiuchi...

Tomoe was assigned a post in Kakiuchi, the last place her father ever visited. She was appointed to the honorary position of assistant director without the need to worry about the scorn of other officers.
She felt that even if she protested, it wouldn't get overturned. She didn't care about any of the faction disputes.
Because she had her own purpose. She was able to work toward that without anger or disappointment, and without needing to put on a bold front.
And yet... that purpose was being taken away from her?!
No matter how well her investigation results were being evaluated, this was taking away the vision of hope Tomoe gleaned when she first came to Kakiuchi.
"...That's true."
Before long.
Yamaoki drew a deep breath and drooped his shoulders before breaking the silence to address Tomoe and attempt to calm her down.
"I could have refused the request.@k@vS11/30/420200243. ...But, I didn't."
"...Why not?!"

"Public Relations Director Maeno is the hope of the career group and a future executive candidate.@k@vS11/30/420200245. A talented man, likely to become the secretariat to the commissioner of the National Police Agency in the future."
"Having a man like that learn of your name and abilities should certainly be valuable for your own career advancement."

"...This is for my sake? What?!@k@vS11/28/420101297. What about this is for my benefit?!@k@vS11/28/420101298. I never asked anybody for anything like this!!@k@vS11/28/420101298_1. How is anything more important than finding my father's killer?!!"
Tomoe tossed the letter down and spat those words out with the intensity on the verge of vomiting blood.
She was shouting emotionally and no longer trying to maintain appearances.
...Even though she believed in him.
No matter how much she fought with her sister, she still believed this man was an ally who truly understood her!
But in the end, was that just a false assumption?!
Was he simply keeping up appearances... and not making any actual effort to understand how she felt?!
Was he just letting her play around under the scope of his own power in order to conceal his guilt at being unable to capture her father's murderer?!
Was that what this was?!

"Do you think career advancement makes me happy?@k@vS11/28/420101300. Where's the justice in that? Don't make me laugh!@k@vS11/28/420101301. You... you're a hypocrite!@k@vS11/28/420101302. You know my father's case is at a deadlock, so you're just hiding that for your own self-interest under the pretense of helping me!"

"Besides, I didn't join the police force to achieve greatness to begin with!@k@vS11/28/420101304. I didn't do it to be chosen for the recommended group!@k@vS11/28/420101305. All of that was for your own self-satisfaction, wasn't it?!!"
"Don't you go overestimating yourself, greenhorn!!@k@vS11/30/420200248. The world is not so soft that one as powerless and devoid of supporters as you can speak so innocently of justice!!"
That was the very first time... Yamaoki raised his voice in anger.
Without thinking... Tomoe recoiled back into the sofa behind her.
...She was scared.
And more than that... she was sad.
She was able to continue venting her anger on Yamaoki to this extent because she was subconsciously reacting like a spoiled child... but after he said that, she just felt sad.
And... she felt helpless... Lost to despair.
No matter how passionately you feel, or how hard you work... when confronted by a massive organization... is there really no value to personal thoughts and convictions...?
That moment... tears began spilling out.
...She already knew. As a member of society, it was unfit for someone of her age to cry.
But she just couldn't stop... And then, Tomoe realized.
No matter how much time had actually passed... time had stood still for her ever since that day.
Even when she acted grandiose and behaved aggressively, even when she engaged in intellectual debates... Tomoe Minai hadn't changed at all since she was 17 years old.
How shameful... She was no different from a spoiled brat throwing a tantrum, was she...?!
"...@k@vS11/30/420200250.I'm sorry for shouting."
"No... I... shouldn't have cried...!"
Tomoe stared straight at Yamaoki with her eyes still moist.
Yamaoki picked up the letter that had fallen to the floor, dusted it off, and offered it to Tomoe with a bitter smile while thinking back on her indomitable spirit.
"Chief Inspector Tomoe Minai.@k@vS11/30/420200252. You've been ordered to transfer to the public relations office of the National Police Agency, starting next month."
"Repeat it."
"...@k@vS11/28/420101311....Tomoe Minai is being transferred to the public relations office of the National Police agency.@k@vS11/28/420101312. ...And that's an order."
"Mmm.@k@vS11/30/420200255. In that case, you may go now."
"...Please excuse me."
And then, She grabbed the letter containing her orders and left the chief's office without looking back.
changeReason: Old 'Tomoe grabbed the letter' vs new 'She grabbed the letter'; drops the character name 'Tomoe' present in JPN 巴は.

TextENGNew: Yamaoki returned to his desk, then picked up the file sitting there and opened it.
It was a formal letter of complaint against Assistant Director Tomoe Minai, submitted under the names of Deputy Chief Hata and Second Division Section Chief Okabe.
"What overblown accusations... Philistines."
He nearly spat the words out, recognizing the vulgarity and hostility between the lines. The contents were subjective and emotional, so any examiner receiving it would be embarrassed just reading it.
In all likelihood, those men were using it as a means to get rid of Tomoe, since she was otherwise outside their reach.

Although, even though the examiner at prefectural police HQ was from the same public security background as Yamaoki, there was no guarantee they'd have the superficial level of wisdom necessary to recognize that.
This complaint was secretly transferred to Yamaoki by a former colleague and friend.

"Anyway, this is just my own personal opinion, off the record.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2002. It's possible to just ignore this as the scribbles of children, but if you leave it alone it could hurt the girl.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2003. ...For now, you should raise a hand."

When it comes to the examiner, it's possible to downplay this or hide it, and it could just become a minor power struggle between them. But this time, there's also the possibility this issue could run without limits.
And it could cast suspicion against Yamaoki's relationship with Madoka and his selection of Tomoe for the recommended group. ...In which case, it could end up hurting the sisters the most.
With that in mind, Yamaoki decided it would be best to transfer Tomoe to another department until things cooled down.
So, the letter from public relations director Maeno was actually a godsend.
...But Yamaoki couldn't tell Tomoe that.
As obstinate as she was, if he told her about this she wouldn't hesitate to fight the accusation.
And even if she proved the accusation false, it would deepen the suspicion that she wasn't assigned to this post on her own merits...
...No, that wasn't the only problem.
If that was all it was, it was at a level she could manage on her own.
But more importantly, if the examiner were to focus on Yamaoki...

"Now, this is all strictly confidential.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2005. But somehow, some of the people at the public safety division of the Metropolitan police department has their eyes on that young lady.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2006. Furthermore, they've gotten wind of this accusation, and it seems they're thinking of using her as bait or something."
"Public safety division?@k@vS11/30/420200259. What on earth could they be planning?"

"I don't know.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2008. But I have a feeling it's connected to the incident she was investigating.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2009. Or the "case" you were pursuing.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2010. It's a strange move for the personnel division to cooperate with the security bureau of the National Police Agency."
The public safety division wouldn't leak intelligence information to another organization, even a fellow police organization... Doing so would invite insider threats.
And it wasn't typical for the security police to rely on information possessed by the personnel division either.
...But they almost always target police officers suspected of leaking information related to national scandals.
Nevertheless, why would they be targeting someone like Tomoe, who was only investigating domestic cases that had no ties to foreign affairs...?
No, something did ring a bell.
Even if it wasn't related to her, there was a case she was continuing to investigate even now. And so, those guys were...!
There was still a huge cloud of darkness around the case Tomoe's father was investigating.
Enough so that Yamaoki still hesitated to tell her the full truth...
In other words, the folks at public security were "laying bait" in the water Tomoe was swimming in, hoping to get rid of her...?!
A horrible thought came to mind. What if the people at public security wanted to set up a workplace accident...? In other words, they wanted to somehow cause harm to Tomoe.
What if she was attacked... or even killed? Was that what they were hoping for?
If they took advantage of the opportunity gleaned from an accident like that, they'd have good reason for intervening.
Yamaoki simply couldn't afford to overlook the possibility...!
Members of the security police couldn't even reveal their mission details to their colleagues, and it was their duty to do even inhumane things in the interest of national security...!

"...For now, it's best to keep the young lady out of the field.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2012. If things go poorly... she could be eliminated.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_guchi2013. ...Didn't you swear in front of Minai's grave that you would make those girls happy?"
"...Hey, Yuusuke.@k@vS11/30/420200261. It seems like I won't be able to avenge you.@k@vS11/30/420200262. In which case, is it wrong for me... to just want to save her?"
...Yamaoki sighed alone, while gazing at the birds through the blinds.
He, too, felt the same as her... He hated being a powerless individual facing down a massive organization.
No... he may have felt it even more strongly than she did.
Fujita waved his hand as he called out to Tomoe... but once he caught a good look at her, he caught his breath.
She was crying. ...The Tomoe Minai he knew had red, swollen eyes.
Even though he thought it was discourteous, he felt embarrassed and speechless upon witnessing such a gallant and strong-minded woman crying.
"M-Minai-san..."
"...S-sorry, Fujita-kun.@k@vS11/28/420101315. My allergies have been really bad these days... Don't look, ok?"
"Ah, s-sorry..."
Fujita looked away with a bitter smile on his face while Tomoe wiped her eyes and nose with a handkerchief. ...Of course he knew she was lying.

"...That's right.@k@vS11/31/421200067. They say there's a huge amount of pollen spreading this year.@k@vS11/31/421200068. Oh, if it's giving you trouble, I know some Chinese herbal remedies. I'll bring you some next time."
"Sure, thanks.@k@vS11/28/420101317. ...Anyway, did you need something?"
Tomoe pinched her nose with a tissue from her pocket, then turned to face him again. When Fujita saw that, he said, "Oh, that's right," and pulled an envelope out of his jacket pocket.
"First of all... here.@k@vS11/31/421200070. The postal clerk brought this earlier. It's addressed to you."
When Tomoe saw the sender's name... her eyes opened wide.
It was from the recently deceased, Nagisa Ozaki...
"Judging by the postage date, it was apparently sent two days before her death.@k@vS11/31/421200072. ...I didn't read the contents."
"I see. ...Thanks."
It was in a lovely envelope, most likely a private message to her. With that in mind, Tomoe slipped it into her jacket pocket intending to read it later.

"Next, there's a report from Hayami-san.@k@vS11/31/421200074. ...Regarding the death of Nagisa Ozaki, an injection mark was found on her left arm.@k@vS11/31/421200075. They still haven't determined what drug she was injected with."
"What's more, there were signs that she was being transported in a vehicle and either was thrown out or jumped out into the road mid-transit."
"Then, she was thrown or fell while the car was still running?"
"Yes, there were scuff marks on both her elbows and forearms, and several places on her thighs too. And her left ankle was sprained."
"And apparently the victim had been moving around for a while after falling onto the road. Several bloodstains were found a few meters from the place where she fell."
Perhaps she was dragging her legs in a state of semi-consciousness, trying to get out of the road. It was clear that the direction she was moving in was toward the sidewalk.

"After that, she fell unconscious at the site we discovered her at, either due to a concussion or drug-induced coma.@k@vS11/31/421200080. ...The forensics team said that was difficult to determine since her cranium was damaged."
"There is a high possibility that it was only coincidence that the body was in such a dimly lit section of the road."

"A joint investigation is being established so that prefectural HQ and Kakiuchi Station can collaborate on this case.@k@vS11/31/421200083. It seems that those punks at the Second Division are taking control, as usual...@k@vS11/31/421200084. What should we do, Minai-san?"
...As Fujita said that, he was waiting for Tomoe to step in and say, "I'll take care of it."
In fact, he had already talked it over with Section Chief Yamamura. And Hanada and Takayashiki had also offered to help.
At first, they simply approached Tomoe out of mere curiosity, offering to be her "bodyguards"... but now, they completely sympathized with her.
However.
"...Let's leave it to them.@k@vS11/28/420101324. It'll just be a hassle even if we make an uproar about them swooping in on us again."
Fujita was at a loss for words after hearing that unthinkable response out of Tomoe.
What did it mean...?
What on earth happened to the burning rage and vast sorrow she was displaying at the crime scene?
"M-Minai-san...?"

"Thank you for taking the trouble to report that.@k@vS11/28/420101326. ...For now, it looks like the prefectural police will be taking the lead on this matter.@k@vS11/28/420101327. Doesn't seem like there's any room for us, so let's wait for them to tell us the results."
"Are you ok with that, Minai-san?@k@vS11/31/421200088. The First Investigation Division is all lined up and ready to take your orders."
Tomoe's heart shook at that massive temptation.
If she gave the order, Fujita would go out and meet with prefectural police HQ immediately.
Honestly, he went so far above and beyond for her as a subordinate... no, as a partner.
On top of that, Hanada, Takayashiki, Andou... pretty much the entire First Division recognized the young newcomer as a comrade... and respected her.
So, if there was anything they could do to help, they were on board... If she asked them to kneel before her, they would comply.
But... she couldn't. She couldn't do that anymore.
Because her position, the power to command them... was already gone...
"...Thank you very much.@k@vS11/28/420101329. You can go home for today.@k@vS11/28/420101330. Give my regards to your wife."
"H-hold on..."
"Let me be alone!!@k@vS11/28/420101332. ...Please..."
After saying that.
Fujita watched his boss solemnly turn her back... and walk away.
Tomoe went down the stairs past the First Investigation Division in order to avoid his gaze.
Dear Detective Minai-sama.
Thank you very much for working so hard and doing so much for me the other day.
I've tried calling you several times since then, but it was always bad timing and you were out, so I've decided to send a letter instead.
In fact, I have something to report.
I was able to talk with Reina over the phone the other day.
It's hard to believe, but I wasn't the one who made the phone call.
Reina was the one who called me.
But it was all too sudden for me, and maybe for Reina too, so it felt a little awkward...
After all this time we didn't know what to talk about, so unfortunately we didn't talk much.
...But we decided that we should meet up again so we can talk more leisurely.
Why did she cause that incident?
Why did she leave without saying a word about it?
And what did we think of each other now, after living far apart...?
Those are the things I'm thinking of asking when we meet up.
Of course, that may very well reopen old wounds for both Reina and me.
And there's still a chance that meeting her ultimately won't be for the best, so I'm still worried I may regret it.
But I still want to start out by talking with Reina again.
Atonement, confessions...? Those no longer matter to me.
We can talk, and argue, and share everything... but I still want to ask Reina to be my friend.
And I want to make sure I can truly become friends with her this time.
Because I still love Reina.
Reina did cause me sadness, but there are definitely a lot of fun memories we had together.
I want to communicate that to her.
And I want to be able to accept her despite the problems and the pain she caused.
Just like she did for me, so long ago...
Do you think she'll laugh when we met up?
Does she still have that particular speaking habit?
I'm really nervous... but simultaneously, I'm full of anticipation.
So I'll believe, and I'll meet with Reina.
And I want to do my best so I can give you good news, Detective.
I'll call you later to share the results.
In case you're busy and I have trouble getting in touch with you again, I wrote down my home phone number. I'd be happy to receive a call any time you'd like.
Anyway, that's all.
Next time we meet, I'll bring you the most beautiful flowers from the gardening club, so look forward to it.
Nagisa Ozaki.
"...Waaaaaaaaaaah!!@k@vS11/28/420101334. Aaaaaaaaaahh!!@k@vS11/28/420101335. ...Damn it, damn iiiit!!!"
I couldn't do anything... I couldn't help her at all!!
Even though I spoke so boldly, I... I...!!!
